tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] Define Workspace entity + WorkspaceProvider + WorkspaceManager interfaces (Zod) in packages/shared
tasks.description:
Create `packages/shared/src/workspace.ts`: the `Workspace` entity Zod schema (id, projectKey, key, path, source, branch, provider 'worktree'|'clone', status, portBase, portBlockSize, hostname (reserved null), url (reserved null), pinned, createdAt, lastUsedAt) + inferred type; the `WorkspaceProvider` port interface (`type`, `create`, `remove`, `isDirty`); the `WorkspaceManager` interface (`ensure`, `list`, `get`, `resolveByPath`, `remove`, `setPinned`, `sweep`); and the request/result types (EnsureWorkspaceRequest, WorkspaceCreateRequest, ProviderResult, DirtyState, RemoveResult, SweepResult, WorkspaceWithSessions). Export via a `@dorkos/shared/workspace` subpath. TSDoc on every export (hard rule #4). The three named dev ports derive from portBase by fixed offset (DORKOS_PORT+0, VITE_PORT+1, SITE_PORT+2) — document this on the schema.

Acceptance: schema parses valid/invalid fixtures; types exported from the barrel; `pnpm typecheck`/`lint` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Defining the Workspace entity and provider/manager interfaces

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] Add the Drizzle workspaces table + migration (derived cache)
tasks.description:
Add `packages/db/src/schema/workspace.ts` with a `workspaces` sqliteTable mirroring the entity (id PK, projectKey, key, path UNIQUE, source, branch, provider, status, portBase int, portBlockSize int, hostname, url, pinned boolean, createdAt, lastUsedAt). Export from the schema barrel. Generate the Drizzle migration into `packages/db/drizzle`. Follow the `agents` table idiom exactly (mesh.ts).

Acceptance: migration applies on a fresh db; `createDb` + `runMigrations` produce the table; `pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the workspaces Drizzle table and migration

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] workspace-store: file-first write-through (sidecar manifest + DB cache)
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/services/workspace/workspace-store.ts`: read/write the per-workspace sidecar manifest `<root>/<projectKey>/<key>.workspace.json` (atomic temp+rename, à la manifest.ts) as the source of truth, and upsert the `workspaces` DB row (onConflictDoUpdate) as the derived cache. Write order: manifest FIRST, then DB (ADR-0043). Removal: delete manifest first, then DB row. Provide `getByPath`/`getByKey`/`list` reads from the cache. Use `resolveDorkHome` for the default root (config.workspace.rootPath override); never `os.homedir()`.

Acceptance: unit tests assert write-through order, atomic write, manifest↔row round-trip, removal order.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] port-allocator: contiguous-block allocation (lowest free block)
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/services/workspace/port-allocator.ts`: allocate the lowest free contiguous block from a pool (base = config.workspace.portBase default 4250, step = portBlockSize default 10) by reading allocated portBase values from the workspaces cache. Disjoint blocks → collisions structurally impossible. Provide `allocate()` and `release(portBase)`; expose the derived named ports (DORKOS_PORT/VITE_PORT/SITE_PORT) for a block. Handle pool exhaustion with a clear error.

Acceptance: unit tests for lowest-free selection, disjointness across N allocations, release-then-reuse, exhaustion error.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] WorkspaceProvider impls: worktree + clone
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/services/workspace/providers/worktree.ts` and `providers/clone.ts`. worktree: `git worktree add <path> -b dork/<key>` from a local `source` checkout; remove via `git worktree remove` (+optional branch delete); `isDirty` = porcelain status + untracked + unpushed (`@{upstream}..`). clone: `git clone <url> <path>` + checkout `dork/<key>`; remove via dirty-checked rm -rf; same isDirty. Both validate the target path with `validateBoundary` against the workspace root before any write. Copy the repo-owned `copy` files into a fresh checkout.

Acceptance: integration tests against a temp git repo fixture: worktree add/remove/isDirty correctness (clean vs uncommitted vs untracked vs unpushed); clone path covered with a local bare-repo fixture.
tasks.activeForm: Implementing the worktree and clone providers

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] hooks executor: Symphony's four hooks
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/services/workspace/hooks.ts`: execute `after_create`/`before_run`/`after_run`/`before_remove` from the repo-owned `.dork/workspace.json` via `sh -lc`, cwd = workspace path, 60s default timeout, env carrying the allocated port block. `after_create`/`before_run` fatal (throw → caller marks workspace failed/aborts); `after_run`/`before_remove` logged-and-ignored. Sanitize+truncate hook output to 2KB before logging. Add a Zod schema for `.dork/workspace.json` (provider, copy[], hooks{}).

Acceptance: unit tests for fatal-vs-ignored semantics, timeout enforcement, 2KB truncation, missing-config tolerance (no hooks → no-op).

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] WorkspaceManager impl: ensure/list/get/resolveByPath/remove/setPinned/sweep
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/services/workspace/workspace-service.ts` composing store + allocator + providers + hooks. `ensure(req)` is reuse-or-create keyed by (projectKey,key): return existing `ready` workspace (bump lastUsedAt) else provision (sanitize key → path → allocate block → manifest 'provisioning' → provider.create → after_create → patch workspace .env with the block → 'ready'). `resolveByPath(absPath)` = the cwd→workspace lookup (path-prefix under workspace root, canonical) powering the session indicator. `remove(id,{force})` and `sweep()` call provider.isDirty and REFUSE on dirty unless force; `sweep` skips pinned and applies terminal-state + retentionCap/age. Key sanitization to [A-Za-z0-9._-]. Export the singleton from `index.ts`.

Acceptance: covered by the validation-criteria integration tests in 1.10.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] workspace-reconciler + bootstrap registration in index.ts
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/services/workspace/workspace-reconciler.ts`: on the existing 5-min unref'd cadence, for each cached row — if the checkout dir is gone, remove the stale row; if the manifest differs from the row, sync manifest→row. Never deletes a checkout. Wire construction + the reconciler start into `apps/server/src/index.ts` (after db creation, following the runtime-registry/mesh pattern), passing dorkHome + db.

Acceptance: unit test reconciles a hand-edited manifest and a missing-checkout row; server boots with the service registered.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the reconciler and bootstrap wiring

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] Add the workspace config section + migration
tasks.description:
Add the `workspace` section to `UserConfigSchema` (`packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts`): { enabled default true, rootPath nullable default null, portBase default 4250, portBlockSize default 10, defaultProvider 'worktree', retentionCap nullable default null }. Add a semver-keyed backfill migration in config-manager per the `adding-config-fields` skill. Verify the `z.toJSONSchema` bridge accepts it.

Acceptance: config-manager loads with the new section; default resolution test; migration is idempotent; `pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the workspace config section and migration

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P1] Integration tests for the 3 validation criteria + unit suite
tasks.description:
Author the acceptance suite for the service: VC#1 — `ensure` two distinct keys → distinct paths + DISJOINT port blocks (and concurrent ensure never overlaps); VC#2 — `ensure` same key twice → same id/path (reuse, lastUsedAt bumped) across simulated sessions/attempts; VC#3 — `remove`/`sweep` REFUSE on uncommitted/untracked/unpushed, succeed only with force, `sweep` skips pinned. Plus write-through order, reconciler sync, key sanitization, boundary rejection. Use a temp git repo fixture; purpose comments; failable assertions.

Acceptance: all three validation criteria pass; `pnpm test -- --run` green for the workspace suite.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P2] /api/workspaces routes
tasks.description:
Create `apps/server/src/routes/workspaces.ts`: GET / (list + attached sessions, opt ?projectKey), GET /:id, POST / (ensure), DELETE /:id (?force), POST /:id/pin, GET /resolve?path= (cwd→workspace), POST /ports (allocate block for a path — the script fallback endpoint). Zod-validate inputs; `force` never inferred. Mount in the app. Attached sessions = sessions whose resolved cwd is under workspace.path (from session metadata).

Acceptance: supertest covers each route incl. dirty-refusal 4xx and resolve hit/miss; `/api/docs` lists them.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P2] Transport verbs + HttpTransport + DirectTransport adapters
tasks.description:
Add the workspace read+write verbs to the `Transport` interface (`packages/shared/src/transport.ts`); implement in `HttpTransport` (calls /api/workspaces/*) and `DirectTransport` (in-process service calls). Keep the FSD/transport idioms.

Acceptance: both adapters typecheck against the interface; a DirectTransport unit test exercises list/ensure/resolve.
tasks.activeForm: Adding Transport verbs and both adapters

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P2] Opt-in workspaceKey session binding (additive, non-breaking)
tasks.description:
Add optional `workspaceKey?` + `provider?` to the send-message/session-create input schema (`SendMessageRequestSchema`). In the sessions route → `trigger-turn`: if workspaceKey present, `workspaceManager.ensure(...)` then set effective cwd = workspace.path (override) + run before_run + ensure ports in the workspace .env. If absent, the path is byte-for-byte unchanged. Document that orchestrated workspace-bound sessions must not use EnterWorktree (boundary §).

Acceptance: integration test — workspaceKey present overrides cwd + injects ports; absent leaves existing behavior identical (regression guard).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring opt-in workspace binding into the session path

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P2] worktree-setup.sh: server-consult + hash fallback
tasks.description:
Add a server-consultation step to `.claude/scripts/worktree-setup.sh`: POST the pwd to `/api/workspaces/ports`, use the returned block (DORKOS_PORT/VITE_PORT/SITE_PORT) when the server responds 200; on any failure (unreachable/non-200/timeout) fall back to the existing hash-mod-150 derivation UNCHANGED. The server becomes the authority when present; the script never hard-depends on it.

Acceptance: script uses server block when reachable (mocked), falls back cleanly when not; existing worktree creation still works offline.
tasks.activeForm: Making worktree-setup.sh consult the server with a hash fallback

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P3] Visual-companion (/design-sync) design pass for the workspace UI
tasks.description:
Run the `/design-sync` visual-companion pass (with the operator) to design the minimal-interactive components against the frozen data shape: the current-workspace indicator badge (session view), and the per-project Workspaces view rows (key, provider, status, port block, pinned/dirty, attached sessions). Requires the operator's claude.ai/design login. Output: agreed component designs to implement in 3.2–3.4.

Acceptance: operator-approved component designs; placement decided (dedicated /workspaces route vs /agents tab).
tasks.activeForm: Designing the workspace UI with the visual companion

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P3] entities/workspace + WorkspaceBadge (the session-view indicator — headline)
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/entities/workspace/`: the Workspace model, TanStack Query hooks (useWorkspaces, useWorkspaceForSession via /resolve), and the `WorkspaceBadge` — the current-workspace indicator shown in the session view (key + project, provider/ports on hover, 'main checkout' fallback when cwd is unmanaged). Consume it in the existing session widget. FSD layering respected.

Acceptance: badge resolves + renders the bound workspace and the fallback; query hooks tested with a mock Transport.
tasks.activeForm: Building the workspace entity and session-view indicator

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P3] Workspaces view widget (per-project list + attached sessions)
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/workspaces-view/` (+ route per the 3.1 decision): per-project/agent grouped list, each workspace row showing key, provider, status, port block, pinned/dirty, and the list of attached sessions (each linking to its session view). Responsive (mobile/tablet/desktop), design-system compliant, motion for transitions.

Acceptance: renders grouped workspaces with attached sessions from a mock Transport; empty + failed states present.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P3] features/workspace-management: pin/remove actions
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/workspace-management/`: pin/unpin and remove actions calling the Transport. Remove surfaces the dirty-refusal as an explicit confirm-with-force dialog (never a silent destroy).

Acceptance: actions call the transport; dirty-remove shows the force-confirm dialog; tested with a mock Transport.

tasks.subject: [workspace-manager] [P4] Draft the two ADRs + contributing guide
tasks.description:
Via `/adr:from-spec`: ADR-A 'WorkspaceProvider — a hexagonal port for isolated workspaces' (mirrors AgentRuntime/Transport; binding is cwd; runtime-worktree boundary); ADR-B 'The DorkOS server is the port authority' (contiguous block allocation replaces branch-name hashing; gtr hash is the offline fallback). Add a `contributing/` guide for the WorkspaceManager and update the worktree memory/dev-workflow note (server is now the port authority).

Acceptance: both ADRs in decisions/ + manifest.json; contributing guide added; `/adr:curate` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Drafting the ADRs and contributing guide
